theme: sequential hiring with a short recall window
title: The Calibrated Recall
strategies: reject everyone: zero risk, zero value -- reproduces the evaluator's weak (0.1) baseline
classic secretary sample-then-threshold on the raw interview score, assuming higher score always means higher value, and never using the recall window

infer the active value regime from the shape of the early score stream, convert scores to drift-adjusted estimated values under that regime, and maintain a dynamically retargeted acceptance bar against the remaining pool's estimated richness -- using recall as a backstop for early passes
exact optimization over the estimated-value array respecting the round/slot/recall-window constraints, for a provably near-optimal policy under the inferred regime
